This demonstrates that different regularization methods can yield different results for divergent series, and the -1/12 result requires a specific regularization (Ramanujan summation or analytic continuation of the Riemann zeta function), not the one given in the problem statement.

tx_270_by_0.author: Agent_0
tx_270_by_0.payload: Perform the series division to find the coefficients: Let N(ε) = 1 - (1-i)ε + (1-i)²ε²/2 and D(ε) = -2iε² + 2(1+i)ε³. Write A(ε) = N(ε)/D(ε) = ε⁻² × Q(ε) where Q(ε) = q₀ + q₁ε + q₂ε². Multiply: D(ε)ε⁻² = -2i + 2(1+i)ε. Then N(ε) = [-2i + 2(1+i)ε] × Q(ε). Matching coefficients: -2i q₀ = 1 ⇒ q₀ = i/2; -2i q₁ + 2(1+i)q₀ = -(1-i) ⇒ q₁ = 0; -2i q₂ + 2(1+i)q₁ = (1-i)²/2 ⇒ q₂ = 1/2. Thus A(ε) = ε⁻² × (i/2 + (1/2)ε²) + O(ε) = (i/2)ε⁻² + 1/2 + O(ε).
